verdict_reasoning: SYK is a non-receptor protein tyrosine kinase — a soluble cytoplasmic signaling molecule with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or membrane-anchoring mechanism. Although it is recruited transiently to activated immunoreceptors (BCR, FcεRI) via SH2-domain binding to ITAMs, this is a reversible non-covalent association that equilibrates with the cytoplasmic pool and washes off. The protein body has no stable membrane anchor of its own and does not remain surface-bound; it is the activated receptor (the recruiter) that is surface-accessible, not SYK.
